hints.Lucerna: 'Lucerna' is the predicate nominative — 'your word' IS 'a lamp'
hints.pedibus: 'pedibus' is dative/ablative plural — it cannot be the subject

hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'lucerna' would be 'lucerna' (same form), but here it's predicate nominative
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'lucerna' would be 'lucerna' (same form), but the speaker isn't addressing a lamp

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'lucerna' with -a ending is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — 'lucerna' is a typical 1st declension -a noun
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'lucerna' is 1st declension

hints.Ablative: 'pedibus' could be ablative plural (same form), but dative of advantage fits — a lamp for my feet

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'pes' with genitive 'pedis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'pes' with consonant stem 'ped-' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us with genitive -us — 'pes' has genitive 'pedis'

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'mei' (masc.) — 'meis' is dative/ablative plural
hints.Ablative: 'meis' could be ablative, but it agrees with 'pedibus' which is dative here

hints.Accusative: 'verbum' could be accusative (same form), but it's the subject — your word IS a lamp

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'verbum' with -um ending is 2nd declension neuter
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — '-um' is characteristic of 2nd declension neuter
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'verbum' is 2nd declension neuter

hints.Accusative: 'tuum' neuter nom. and acc. are identical, but it agrees with subject 'verbum' — nominative

translation: Your word is a lamp to my feet
